observations.recommendation:

We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting journal entries to recognize the cost of the infrastructure projects transferred by the DPWH to the LGU with the corresponding depreciation cost in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer  commented that constant follow-ups and coordination with  the DPWH was made, however, the verification process is not yet  completed due to lack of manpower.
observations.reason: The projects are still in the process of verification by the engineering departments. Likewise, detailed engineering activities of the  projects were not sufficiently undertaken as of this period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Create a plantilla position for the GSO who will be responsible in the monitoring and management of the properties of the LGU:

Direct the Inventory Committee to:

Conduct physical count of all PPE of the LGU, whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station: and

Affix property tags to each property and equipment during the physical inventory for proper control and monitoring.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to :

Update the property records based on the results of physical count, and reconcile with the accounting records:

Make use of the ICS in the issuance of property and equipment below ₱50,000.00 for proper accountability and control:

Maintain/update property cards and

Maintain and update the PAR to be renewed every three years or every time there is a change in custodianship of the property.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant:

To maintain and update PPE Ledger Cards and reconcile with Property Cards of the Municipal Treasurer:

To record in the books of accounts donated properties based on available supporting documents:

To reclassify unserviceable properties recorded as PPE to appropriate asset account and verify the where about of none existing properties still recorded in the books and identify the persons accountable therefor based on available PAR or other records for determining accountability for those properties.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are understaffed, hence, the audit recommendations were remained unimplemented.
observations.reason: The proposed creation of GSO is not yet undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all procurements of supplies and materials as inventory following the Perpetual Inventory Method and maintain SLC’s

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to monitor and control the inventory of supplies and materials through the issuance of RIS and maintenance of stock cards for supplies:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate weekly the RIS issued and facilitate the preparation of SSMI which will be basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the consumption and/or distribution to the appropriate expenses accounts and

Create a plantilla position for the General Services Officer (GSO) who will take charge of controlling and monitoring the purchase and issuances of all supplies and material of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
Management commented that the reason for their non-compliance is due to lack of manpower and  system, and 
stockroom for inventory.
observations.reason: The proposed creation of GSO is not yet undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the unauthorized deduction form the proceeds of the RPT share of the barangays:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to cause the refund of the unauthorized charging of overtime pay, office supplies expenses and travelling expenses by the concerned officials and employees in the amount of  ₱1,061,119.62 and

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply in the release of barangay shares from RPT to each barangay on a quarterly basis within five days at the end of each quarter in order to augment their respective appropriations for projects and programs beneficial to their constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the concerned office had already refrained from such practice.
observations.reason: Though the concerned office already stopped the unauthorized deduction from the proceeds of the RPT share of the barangays, a total amount of ₱1,061,119.62 is not fully refunded yet by the concerned employees. Partial payment of ₱195,352.00 was made per OR No. 9643739 dated October 9, 2024 amounting to ₱65,000.00; and OR No. 9643746 dated October 28, 2024 amounting ₱130,352.00.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Stop drawing cash advance for the payment of expenses other than those purposes allowed by existing rules and regulations; and
Strictly adhere to the rules and regulations requiring that all payments be made by check directly to suppliers/creditors in a per transaction basis and not through single check scheme for several expenses;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations on the proper application of cash advances and ensure that payments shall be made by check directly payable to the creditor so that applicable withholding taxes can be deducted and remitted to the national government.

We also recommended that Management exert maximum effort to collect the receivables from the debtors to provide funds for other intended priority projects of the LGU. In case collections thereof could no longer be ascertained, Management may request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable account to clean the account subject to the requirements and procedures of the COA Circular.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to refrain from recommending award of contract to bidders whose registered line of business does not match the goods to be procured.
We, likewise, recommended that Management instruct the BAC and TWG to perform the post-qualification of bidders thoroughly by looking into the permits and licenses necessary in relation to the goods/services to be delivered/performed.
We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to strictly adhere with the procurement procedures set forth by the procurement law.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman, in coordination with the BAC Secretariat, to furnish copies of perfected contract and of all the documents forming part thereof to the Audit Team within five working days upon perfection thereof for review and evaluation.
We recommended that Management secure valid ORs from the contractors of the aforementioned projects procured to properly substantiate the payments made for the projects.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that a budget be allocated for the titling of lands.
We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to exhaust all possible means for the speedy titling of lands and secure Certificate of Title to protect the interest of the LGU from any adverse claims by third parties.

We recommended that Management instruct the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Treasurer to certify only the loan applications of employees whose salaries could accommodate the monthly loan amortization and would still have a net take home pay of not less than ₱5,000.00.

We recommended that Management direct all officers and employees to register their daily attendance in the biometric system and at the same time, maintain logbooks at the desk security unit to support the manual entries in the DTR for verification by the HRMO.

We recommended that Management employ the use of ATM Payroll System for the payment of wages to JO and COS workers to deter possible risks of loss of government funds through misapplication, robbery or other similar incidents.
Likewise, we recommended that Management, through the Human Resource Management Office, coordinate with the authorized government depository bank to facilitate the enrolment of the 244 JO and 158 COS workers to the ATM Payroll accounts

We recommended that Management create a plantilla position for GSO who will take charge of controlling and monitoring the purchases and issuances of all supplies and materials of the LGU.
We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to take charge of the overall functions of the GSO for the meantime and to formulate measures including preparation of documents, canvassing and all other general services functions for uniformity and transparency purposes.
We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to submit promptly the GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Issue demand letters to all delinquent taxpayer and if they failed to settle their obligations, enforce administrative and judicial actions vested under the law;
Post the notice of delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the LGU; and
Seek assistance and intervention of barangay officials in the collection of delinquent accounts by providing them a list of delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a list of unreleased checks at every year-end, if any, to the Accounting Office for the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher ( JEV ) by the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of an updated Report on Real Property Assessment and copy furnish the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPTAR which will be used as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles from real property tax in the financial statement of the Municipality; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to revise and update his report on Certified List of Tax Delinquencies as of December 31, 2021 to reflect the actual amount for transparency.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exercise due diligence in the recording of financial transactions in the books of accounts and in the cashbook, respectively;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that checks were prepared in the correct amount of disbursement; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately adjust or correct any errors discovered in the BRS to present the correct balances of affected accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify the discrepancies noted and submit revised report of total collectibles as of December 31, 2021 to the Audit Team for verification;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to prepare a master list of homeowners and to retrieve all pertinent documents evidencing sale to establish existence and accuracy; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the balance of collectibles from homeowners in the books of accounts as soon as the correct amount of receivables is duly established to fairly present the correct balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate collection of the amounts due, especially item numbers 7, 9 and 10 of the above data, by notifying the borrowers/suppliers concerned through the issuance of demand letters; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to request for write-off of the dormant accounts that can no longer be accounted or settled in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit documents in support of the newly recorded AP for evaluation of the Audit Team, otherwise, derecognize the amount of ₱1,981,446.70 from the books of the Municipality;
Revert to government equity the recorded AP amounting to ₱1,096,205.69 that had been outstanding for more than two years with no valid claims exist; and
Prepare correcting entries on the misclassified accounts in the total amount of ₱3,667,290.85 and negative balance of ₱41,489.50.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries in the amount ₱2,530,440.55 in the books of accounts of the Municipality to present the correct balances of Loans Payable - Domestic and Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Establish the complete details of the fund transfers and to inquire with the source agencies of the existence of the same in their books for the immediate remittance of the unexpended balances to the respective SAs or to the National Treasury in the total amount of ₱1,905,922.87;
Request for write-off of the dormant accounts that can no longer be accounted or settled amounting to ₱1,301,959.89 following the prescribed guidelines and procedures for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement; and
Take-up correcting entries on the misclassified accounts in the total amount of ₱701,141.24 and verify the negative balance of ₱640,557.51 included in the account balance for proper adjustment.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the Statement of Financial Performance by making necessary correcting entries for OMOE and related expense accounts;
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge the appropriate budgetary account by considering the actual nature of the transaction; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that transactions are recorded based on the actual nature and not on the account shown in the obligation request for proper presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections intact and daily or the next banking day with the authorized government depository banks of the Municipality to avoid loss, misuse or misappropriation of government funds;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to re-compute and tally RCD with the duplicate copies of ORs before recording in the books of accounts; and
Cause the Municipal Accountant to prepare correcting entries on the understatement of recorded collections totaling ₱4,014.40 and unrecorded rental income amounting to ₱1,000.00.

We recommended that Management:
Require the designated Human Resource and Management Officer to formulate concrete procedures on the preparation of payroll vouchers including responsibility of every department heads to expedite the preparation thereof;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that amount of cash advances granted is equal to the total net payments indicated in the approved payroll or list of beneficiaries; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no additional advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made through the issuance of a certification stating that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee with a representative per department, if possible, who shall be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory of all PPE of the Municipality and the result of which to be incorporated in the RPCPPE and to be reconciled with the accounting records to determine any discrepancy and a copy of which to be submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the balance of collectibles from homeowners as of December 31, 2020 in the books to fairly present the correct balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.
In a letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Accountant showed willingness to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the outstanding stale checks reflected in the BRS.  We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees of the existence of the checks and to follow the procedures prescribed in the GAM to ensure correctness of recording of cancellation and adjustment in the books of accounts.
In a letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Accountant agreed with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to confer with the depository bank to determine the nature of the reconciling items.  We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately adjust all valid reconciling items reflected in the BRS in the books of accounts of the agency duly supported with documents to present the correct balances of the cash in bank account.
In a letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Accountant commented that he will make the necessary adjusting entries upon determination of the nature of the reconciling items.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the preparation of an updated Report on Real Property Assessment and copy thereof be furnished to the Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR.  We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPTAR which will be used as basis in the setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles from real property tax in the financial statements of the LGU.
In a letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Assessor commented that his department is waiting for the upgrade of their system to facilitate an updated Report on Real Property Assessment.

We recommended that Management cause the immediate collection of the amounts due by notifying the borrowers/suppliers concerned through the issuance of demand letters; otherwise, the Management may instruct the Municipal Accountant to make a request for write-off of the dormant accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
In the letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team to prepare the necessary supporting documents for the request for write-off.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide complete description and cost segregation of road components for all road projects to the Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to provide for the corresponding depreciation costs for all the depreciable PPE accounts of the LGU.
In the letter-reply of the Management, they promised that they will comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to establish the complete details of the fund transfers and to inquire with the SAs of the existence of the same in their books for the immediate remittance of the unexpended balances to the respective SAs or to the National Treasury in conformity with the provision of paragraph 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94–013.
In the letter-reply of the Municipal Accountant, he assured the Audit Team that he will make a confirmation with the SAs if there is an outstanding balance in their books and will prepare the summary of all funds to be remitted to the SAs or to the Bureau Treasury.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries both in the General Fund and Trust Fund books to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  We also recommended that Management ensure that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRMMF for capital outlay shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the projects funded therefore are completed and any savings shall be available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP.
In the letter-reply of the Municipal Accountant, he assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record all the financial transactions of the LGU in accordance with the prescribed Chart of Accounts in order to have accurate presentation of accounts and to give accurate information to the users of the financial statements.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the suggested correcting journal entry as shown in the next page:
Debit		  Credit
Grants and Donations in Kind		₱2,729.59
Due to NGAs					₱2,729.59
In a letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Accountant agreed with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the proper handling of collections and deposit in order to ensure that collections are deposited intact daily or the next banking day with the government depository banks of the agency to avoid loss, misuse or misappropriation of government funds.
In the letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Treasurer commented that he will try his best to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require all Collecting Officers to remit their daily collections to the Municipal Treasurer at the end of each business day to avoid exposure of government funds to possible loss or misuse.  We also recommended that Management consider to designate a Liquidating Officer to perform initial verification on the correctness of collections reported and remitted by the collecting officers.
The Municipal Treasurer commented through her written reply that he will comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record deposits only upon actual deposit was made in the authorized bank account of the LGU.  We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to issue official receipt for every intra-agency transaction to acknowledge receipt thereof.
In the letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Accountant assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Petty Cash Custodian to immediately discontinue the practice of paying regular and recurring expenses out of the PCF.  We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that rules and regulations on the utilization and proper handling of PCF are strictly observed.
The Management assured the Audit Team, through their written reply, of their compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that only the designated disbursing officers perform disbursing functions in order to ensure that all payees in the payroll will be paid and cash advance for payroll fund and cash advance to special disbursing officer be duly accounted.
In a letter reply of the Management, they agreed and promised to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee with adequate number of members who shall be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and the result of which to be reported in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE and to be reconciled with the accounting records to determine any discrepancy.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards for land and for the Municipal Treasurer to maintain complete and updated Property Cards per category of PPE and ensure that the records are reconciled.
In a letter-reply, the Management assured the Audit Team in their compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to reconcile and to account the difference noted in the submitted reports, and to submit a corrected inventory of land as of December 31, 2020 to the Audit Team with complete details and supporting documents.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered parcels of land and facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the LGU.  We, likewise, recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover the expenses on the transfer and registration of the parcels of land.
In the letter-reply of the Management, they commented that they will comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor and enforce the provisions stipulated in the contract specifically the completion of the project on the target date and to submit the Statement of Work Accomplished as of target date of completion.  We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractor to pay the liquidated damages.
In the letter-reply of the Management, they commented that the cause of the deferral was due to the delay of the loan proceeds from Landbank, however, rest assured that they will comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of paying expenses through reimbursements, instead all payments must be made by check in the name of the supplier and payments of petty operating expenses may be made through the petty cash fund or be paid by the disbursing officer through his cash advance in accordance with the rules and regulations provided therein.
In the letter-reply of the Management, they give assurance to the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management instruct the HRMO Designate to require the JOW and COS personnel with no PDS on file to immediately submit their PDS in order to establish their identity and to make sure that all information are properly filled-up.
We also recommended that Management require the HRMO Designate to prepare an organizational structural, staffing plan and manual or policy on hiring JOW and COS and to take into consideration the necessity and nature of work to be done in order to avoid duplication of work of regular employees and incurrence of additional expenditures.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) and BAC Secretariat to submit immediately the contracts and its supporting documents of five projects with total contract cost of ₱9,332,008.88 to the Audit Team and to observe the submission of contracts within five working days from the execution of a contract in order to gain the benefits of facilitating the review and evaluation process.
In the written reply of the Management, they agreed with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal LDRMMO to carefully plan the PPAs that fully support the implementation of disaster risk reduction and management in the LGU.  We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that only PPAs that fully support the purpose of LDRRMF be charged against the fund.
The Management commented in their written reply that rest assured they will comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to formulate PPAs for the 20% DF that contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU, and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only PPAs that attains the purpose of 20% development fund provided under the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-001 be utilized and charged against the fund.
In the letter-reply of the Management, they assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and BAC Office to transmit all paid/claimed vouchers together with all its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for submission to the Audit Team.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to locate the other paid vouchers with all its supporting documents which were not yet submitted and to submit the same to the Audit Team, and to strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of accounts and financial reports within 10 days of the following month to the Audit Team in order to facilitate the establishment of the reliability, propriety and legality of the transactions of the LGU.
The Management commented in their written reply that they will immediately submit the DVs not yet submitted.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to continue and intensify their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending notice of delinquency to taxpayers and forming committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.  If circumstances warrant, adopt the procedures and remedies provided in Sections 254 and 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes.
In the letter-reply of the Management, the Municipal Treasurer commented that they will comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts of the Municipality to present the correct amount of Loans Payable - Domestic account in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
a. Ensure the conduct of complete physical inventory of all municipal
properties and the preparation and submission of the Report on the
Physical Count of PPE, in prescribed format, to the Municipal
Accountant for reconciliation;
b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
perform reconciliation of the actual physical count with the
accounting records and to establish the cause/s of discrepancies and
make appropriate adjustments as warranted;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
maintain complete and updated Property Cards and PPE Ledger
Cards, respectively;
d. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to file and update Property
Acknowledgement Receipt to properly identify the accountable
officer in case of loss and maintain property tags on all properties of
the Municipality; and
e. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide for the corresponding
depreciation costs of the School Buildings, Slaughterhouses and
Agricultural and Forestry Equipment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Assessor to facilitate the preparation of an updated Report on Real Property
Assessment and copy thereof be furnished to the Municipal Treasurer to update
the RPTAR. We also recommended the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the
Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPTAR which will be used as basis in the
setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables to reflect the actual collectibles from real
property tax in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts of
the Municipality to present the correct amount of Loans Payable - Domestic
account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Inform the Property Officer to periodically render Report of Local
Road Networks, and furnish copies to the concerned offices. Also,
Municipal Engineer’s Office should provide the Municipal
Accountant and the Property Officer with the complete description
and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
b. Create an item for General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road
Network Inventory and Road Map and to keep a complete Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary
ledger for each individual road networks; and
d. Ensure coordination among LGU Offices to properly implement the
accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset
Management System.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible means to
document the legality of ownership over the 14 parcels of land through
Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipal Government pursuant to
Section 39 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to ensure strict compliance in the proper handling of collections and
that the same are deposited wholly or intact with the government depository
banks of the agency in accordance with the requirements of PD 1445 and
MNGAS for LGUs to avoid misuse or misappropriation of government funds.

We recommended that Management effect the increase of the fidelity
bond of the Designated Disbursing Officer commensurate with her
accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-
2009, in order to provide adequate protection of government funds.
Likewise, we recommended Management to assign other personnel for
the Petty Cash Fund in order that maximum accountability will be within the
maximum amount of bond.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances on
time; otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for
any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash advances
within the prescribed period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD
1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to provide certification that the
previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the
books in compliance with the documentary requirements required
under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and ensure
that no additional cash advances be granted unless the previous cash
advance was fully liquidated as required under the same COA
Circular;
c. Ensure that claims beyond the allowable rates provided under
Executive Order No. 77 dated March 15, 2019 are absolute necessary
in the performance of the assignment by issuing a certification of the
same; and
d. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amount granted
for advances to officers and employees is the same as the amount
shown in the duly approved Itinerary of Travel, respectively, and
ensure that liquidations of cash advances are duly supported with
documentary requirements as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that approved payroll or list of
payees indicating their net payments should be strictly required before granting
advances for payroll and the amount should be equal to the total amount
indicated therein.

We recommended that Management exert effort to collect the receivables
from debtors to have additional funds which could be used by the Agency for its
operations. In case of non-collection, the agency may request for the write-off
of the dormant accounts in accordance with the requirements and procedures
provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for fair
presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
secure immediately property insurance for the non-insured government assets
and to submit the updated Property Inventory Form (PIF) as basis for the
assessment of general insurance coverage using the format prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 for all insurable assets not later than
April 30 of each year.
Likewise, we also recommended that Management instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to secure insurance only from Government Service
Insurance System (GSIS) as provided in the same COA Circular.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to create Disposal
Committee to take care of the unserviceable and obsolete properties which were
beyond economic repair and dispose said items using appropriate mode of
disposal, to avoid further deterioration and loss its salvage value in accordance
with the procedures and requirements in Sections 76 and 79 of PD 1445.
We also recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the
Inventory Committee and Accounting Office to exert more effort in establishing
the cost of the unserviceable properties in order to dispose these items to avoid
further deterioration.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the
documentary requirements to support the payments of the purchase of land as
prescribed under PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and to submit
immediately the lacking documents enumerated herein. Likewise, we also
recommended that Management submit justification for the advance payment
made on the parcel of lot amounting to ₱320,000.00 located at Barangay
Mancup, Calasiao.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to ensure that only
permanently appointed officials perform disbursing functions and that payment
of wages of laborers, payroll of CNHS personnel, various prizes and honoraria
of Senior Citizen Barangay Presidents be made thru advances for payroll or
advances to special disbursing officer named under a duly designated disbursing
officer in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997
and COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce strict
adherence to the provisions set forth in Sections 4 and 6, Annex E of the 2016
IRR of RA 9184 before granting fifteen percent (15%) advance payment and
before payment of progress billing to contractor to ensure that advance payment
made and uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities are covered
with bond or retention money.

We recommended that Management require the immediate submission
of the lacking documents to the audit team for verification and further audit
action. We also recommended that documentary requirements itemized in
Sections 9.1.1 and 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 be strictly provided by
the contractor before effecting payments thereof to establish validity and
propriety.
Likewise, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure
that warranty security is posted by the contractor before effecting final payment
as provided in Section 62 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of RA 9184, to assure that warranty against structural defects will
be covered by the contractor.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the members of
the Bids and Awards Committee, including the BAC Secretariat:
a. Strictly observe provisions of Sections 2 and 48.2 of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that procurement of goods and services
are advantageous to the Municipality and resort only to alternative
modes of procurement if the conditions are met as provided under
Sections 48, 52 and 53.9 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Social Welfare
Development Officer to submit the following supporting documents
for further audit action:
1. List of actual beneficiaries per feeding program;
2. Schedule of distribution of perishable and non-perishable goods
to Day Care Workers;
3. Duly signed Inspection and Acceptance Report; and
4. Pictures on the actual feeding program

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents to support the claim
for BAC honoraria to avoid the issuance of notice of suspension.
We also recommended that Management strictly follow the provisions of
Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 as amended by Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018
in withholding of taxes on all compensations being received by the officials and
employees of the Municipality, including honoraria, and remit the same to BIR
in accordance with said regulations.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to limit the hiring of experts
to its actual need of consultancy services, taking into consideration the specific
work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special
knowledge or training that cannot be done by the regular staff of the
Municipality.
It is also recommended that the aforementioned documentary
requirements be submitted by the Consultants for further evaluation of the
Audit Team.

We recommended that the Management pay only disbursement voucher
with proper and complete supporting documents to ensure the validity and
propriety of the payment made thereof in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D.
1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately
submit the lacking documents shown in Annex J.1 and J.2 to establish the
validity and propriety of the transactions.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Provide or develop its own engineered sanitary landfill so that it will
not depend on other LGUs for disposal of its residual non-recyclable
wastes;
b. Ensure the establishment of MRF in every barangay or cluster
barangays in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003; and
c. Enjoin the municipal and barangay officials to enforce the
segregation of wastes in their barangays in order to provide a
pollution and health hazard-free environment.

We recommended that the Municipality continue to appropriate at least
5 per cent fund requirement in the annual budget and to incorporate only GAD-
related programs and activities in the GAD Plan and Budget to address gender
issues and concerns affecting the welfare and full development of women in
order to address gender-based inequalities and enable women and men to
equally contribute and benefit from development.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the regulations on
the submission of accounts and financial reports within ten (10) days of the
following month to the Audit Team in order not to delay the verification and
audit of financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort in reconciling the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to identify the discrepancy on the balances thereof and to effect the necessary adjustments to reconcile the two accounts.

Moreover, reconcile the accounting records with the BIR to make sure that no tax withheld remained unremitted to avoid incurrence of penalties in the future.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to give priority attention on
the submission of the required supporting documents in order to make the
necessary adjustment in the books upon approval by proper authorities.

Further, we have recommended that management insure all its
insurable properties of the Municipality in order to minimize the impact of
any such damage, destruction or loss in the operations of the municipal
government.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Inventory Committee to complete the Inventory Report indicating the total
amount of each category of the PPE account to be reconciled with the
accounting records to determine any discrepancy; and to conduct physical
inventory of the PPE under Special Education Fund and Trust Fund. We
also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to keep complete and updated
property cards and property, plant and equipment ledger cards or
subsidiary ledger card for each account of the Property, Plant and
Equipment, respectively, which should reconcile with the corresponding
control account of all the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the
general ledger.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provision of Section 32,
Volume I of MNGAS whereby all collections must be deposited intact and
daily or not later than the next banking day with the authorized depository
bank of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that salary loans
and premiums of municipal employees were correctly withheld from their
salaries before payments are made to national agencies in order to avoid the
recurrence of negative balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
immediate liquidation of the cash advances of the municipal officials and
employees concerned and strictly observe the rules and regulations in the
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance to avoid the recurrence
of the deficiency noted.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to faithfully adhere to the
procedures in the establishment, utilization and replenishment as well as
liquidation of the Petty Cash Fund as provided in Section 4.3.1 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 and Section 48 of MNGAS, Vol. I. We also
recommended that management consider the amount of cash advance for
Petty Cash Fund needed by the Municipality which is sufficient for petty
operating expenses for one month.

We have recommended that the Accounting Office exert diligent
effort to determine the discrepancies between Due from Other Funds account
and Due to Other Funds account and draw the necessary adjusting or
correcting entries to reconcile the two accounts. We also recommended that
the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant effect settlement of the
receivables from other funds and payables to other funds.

We have reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that the
Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and
furnish the Accounting Office the certified list required by Section 20,
Volume I of the MNGAS which shall be the basis in the recording of the
RPT/SET Receivables in the books at the beginning of the year in order to
have a more reliable amount of collectibles from real property tax. We also
recommended that the Local Chie Executive require the Accounting Office to
refrain from adjusting the RPT/SET Receivables account resulting in zero
account balances in the financial statements.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict
compliance to the provisions of Local Budget Circular No. 103 that claims for
RATA be supported with a certification showing the number of workdays of
actual performance in a month issued by the Office of the HRMO for
officials covered under Section 7.1 of LBC No. 103, and by Sanggunian
Secretary for the members of Sanggunian Bayan covered by Section 7.5 of
the same Circular.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the head of
the Local School Board, adhere strictly with the regulation in the
prioritization and utilization of the SEF particularly for the construction and
repair of school buildings and establishment of new extension classes or
acquisition of books and instructional materials of students and teachers as
required in DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and 1A and in
consonance with Sections 100 and 272 of RA 7160 in order to ensure an
effective educational development within the locality.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all
supporting documents are duly certified by them including the Journal
Entry Voucher.

We have recommended that the local government unit require the
creation of municipal ordinance/resolution for the proper segregation of
waste as well as the implementation of solid waste management program and
consider the establishment of MRF in every barangay or cluster barangays
in compliance with the provisions of RA 9003 in order to provide a pollution
and health hazard-free environment. We also recommended that
management enjoin the municipal and barangay officials to enforce the
segregation wastes.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Budget Officer to refrain from charging expenses not related in addressing
gender issues, promote women’s empowerment and gender equality as
provided in the provisions of the Joint Circular of PCW-NEDA-DBM; and to
charge it to the regular Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses
(MOOE) of the LGU.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that
disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentation,
duly certified and signed by officials concerned, payrolls signed by payees
and retention fees are imposed. We have also recommended that the Local
Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat to submit the required
documents in order to complete the contract review and technical evaluation
of projects and ensure that all contracts submitted for review are supported
with complete to avoid the recurrence of the deficiencies noted.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office
indicate the nature of payment on the official receipt issued to contractors
and not the name of the project to determine if payment is for the purchase
of bid documents or for other required fees.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Engineer to issue Certificate of Acceptance only after the one (1)
year defects liability and after conducting final inspection to determine the
presence of any defects for which the concerned contractor may be held
liable according to warranty provisions in the contract in accordance with
the provisions of Section 62 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct: (a)
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to release the share of each
barangay from real property tax within five (5) days after the end of each
quarter in accordance with Section 286(a) of RA 7160 in order for the
barangays to utilize the funds for projects/programs beneficial to barangay
folks; and (b) Municipal Accountant to determine the amount released to the
barangays under the Trust Fund which resulted in the negative balance of
(P10,932.56) under the Trust Fund and draw adjusting entries, if necessary.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Accounting Office to submit the collection reports, disbursement vouchers
and bank reconciliation statements as indicated therein. We also
recommended that the Treasury and Accounting Offices faithfully observe
the submission of accounts and other required reports within the prescribed
period in accordance with the above-mentioned provisions for proper and
timely review thereof.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended
balance of the LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund account to ensure that
the fund shall be used only for the intended purpose; and the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate Registry of
Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations and prepare and submit
through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer the
Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th
day after the end of each month, respectively, in pursuance to the provisions
of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to allocate 1% of the IRA for the implementation of programs on child
delinquency of the LCPC in compliance with the provision of Section 15 of
Chapter I, Title III of R.A. 9344. We also recommended that programs and
activities funded therefrom should address and be geared towards child
protection and ensure proper implementation of programs in order to prevent
juvenile delinquency.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive evaluate the
possible income which could be derived from the operation of the
Slaughterhouse and to consider its utilization in order to collect additional
income therefrom that could be used to pay the loan amortization and
interest with the concerned bank.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit immediately the Cash Local Treasury account balance of P5,335,461.44 in the authorized depository bank of the Municipality and strictly comply with the provision of Section 32, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUS whereby all collections must be deposited intact and daily or not later than the next banking day.
